title: frankenia grandifolia subsp. campestris
intro: Frankenia grandifolia subsp. campestris, also known as the saltbush or frankincense bush, is a shrubby plant in the Frankeniaceae family, characterized by its small, white flowers and salt-excreting leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to possess medicinal properties beneficial for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Frankenia grandifolia subsp. campestris include its ability to reduce inflammation, prevent infection, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable remedy for skin issues and minor injuries. Frankenia grandifolia subsp. campestris can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and topical creams, although its usage should be done under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
